Home › Responsible Gambling

Responsible Gambling

Gambling should be entertaining, not a source of financial stress. This page lists practical resources for South African players who want to manage their gambling or seek support.

National Responsible Gambling Helpline

0800 006 008

Free call · Available 24 hours · 7 days a week

National Responsible Gambling Programme (NRGP)

The NRGP is a South African government-funded programme providing free counselling and support for people affected by gambling-related harm. Services are available in person, by telephone, and online.

Website: www.responsiblegambling.org.za

Signs of Problem Gambling

  • Spending more than you can afford to lose
  • Borrowing money or selling assets to fund gambling
  • Feeling anxious, irritable, or preoccupied with gambling
  • Chasing losses — increasing bets to recover what was lost
  • Neglecting work, family, or other responsibilities to gamble
  • Lying to family or friends about gambling activity
  • Failed attempts to cut down or stop gambling

If you recognise any of these signs in yourself or someone close to you, contact the NRGP helpline: 0800 006 008.

Responsible Gambling Tools at Casinos

All licensed South African online casinos are required to provide the following tools. Use them proactively, not in crisis.

Deposit Limits

Set daily, weekly, or monthly deposit maximums. Increases require a cooling-off period; reductions apply immediately.

Session Time Limits

Set a maximum time per session. The casino logs you out when the limit is reached.

Self-Exclusion

Block yourself from a casino for a defined period (typically 6 months to 5 years). This is a hard block — you cannot reverse it during the exclusion period.

Reality Checks

Periodic notifications showing how long you have been playing and your net wins or losses for the session.

Age Restriction

Gambling is restricted to persons 18 years and older in South Africa. Licensed casinos are required to verify player age before allowing real-money play. If you have a minor in your household, use parental control software to block access to gambling sites.
